programme.desc: Mental Health Television Network is dedicated to changing the way we understand and address mental health. programme.title: Quo Vadis TV
programme.desc: Quo Vadis TV is committed to sharing the Biblical message in a way that makes Christian living practical, realistic, and attainable. Our theme is summarized the question: "where are you going?" Our Aim with this inquiry is to help people find orientation and find the path back to God.

programme.title: Jeopardy!
programme.desc: "Jeopardy!" is a classic game show -- with a twist. The answers are given first, and the contestants supply the questions. Three contestants, including the previous show's champion, compete in six categories and in three rounds (with each round's "answers" being worth more prize money). In the third round, "Final Jeopardy," the contestants can name their own jackpot -- as long as it's within the amount of money they've already earned. If a player finishes the second round with zero dollars, they are eliminated from "Final Jeopardy." The first version of "Jeopardy!," which aired from 1964 to 1975 on NBC, was hosted by Art Fleming. Alex Trebek began with the program in 1984 (at the start of its syndicated run) and hosted until his death in 2020.

programme.title: Switch
programme.desc: Jeff Hephner challenges contestants who are ranked based on their answers to trivia questions for a chance at $10,000; every question counts, as each answer can propel them to the top or to the bottom, where they'll be in jeopardy of elimination.

programme.title: Split Second

programme.title: Tic Tac Dough
programme.desc: Two contestants compete to place three marks in a row on trivia board avoiding a dragon that can steal turns; the winner faces the dragon in a final round where it blocks three-in-a-row attempts to prevent the $10,000 prize win.

programme.title: Bingo Blitz
programme.desc: Contestants compete in a dynamic blend of trivia and bingo.

programme.title: America Says
programme.desc: Game show series have been making a comeback in television, and media and "America Says" is no exception. This program is following in the footsteps of the popular mobile app game "HQ Trivia." The network is planning its own spin on the game show, and Fran Shea, evp, programming and marketing, says she "has been studying the GSN audience and prepared a slate of new shows that reflect what it wants: family-friendly, fun, escapist television." In "America Says," two teams face off to guess Americans' responses to questions covering a variety of topics. Michael Higgins is the host.

programme.title: Family Feud
programme.desc: Steve Harvey assumes the hosting duties for this daytime game show in which five members of one family are pitted against five members of another family. Each team's goal is to guess the results of audience survey questions. A player from each team initially faces off, answering the question to earn the chance to either pass or play. The winning family gets a chance to earn extra cash in a bonus round.

programme.title: Celebrity Wheel of Fortune
programme.desc: "Celebrity Wheel of Fortune" takes a new spin on the iconic game show "Wheel of Fortune." Hosted by pop culture legends Ryan Seacrest and Vanna White, "Celebrity Wheel of Fortune" welcomes celebrity contestants to spin the world's most famous wheel and solve word puzzles for a chance to win up to $1 million. All of the money won by the celebrity contestants goes to a charity of their choice.

programme.title: Celebrity Jeopardy!
programme.desc: Celebrity contestants compete for a chance to win money for a charity of their choice.

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Everyday people battle a variety of trivia questions and a 40-foot wall for a chance to win millions of dollars. Each pair of deserving contestants, from siblings to spouses to best friends, has a plan to use the life-changing winnings for good things. But defeating the wall is no easy feat. Executive Producer LeBron James' game is a true test of both knowledge and poise. With momentary changes between fortune and failure, the outcome is as unpredictable as the bounce of the ball.

programme.title: Family Feud Favorites
programme.desc: "Family Feud" has seen some crazy moments over the years, so buckle up as we revisit these fan favorites. This fast-paced clip show delivers the funniest, most viral and most unforgettable highlights from America's favorite game show. From hilariously wrong answers to Steve Harvey's one-of-a kind reactions, "Family Feud Favorites" captures the best moments that fans can't stop talking about.

programme.title: Hope and Health With Mathew Embry
programme.desc: Louise Zylstra was a professional golfer when she was diagnosed with multiple sclerosis, and she responded the only way she knew how: by pushing harder. She trained for hours a day trying to overpower MS, only to drive her body into severe inflammation and neurological symptoms that left her barely able to walk. In this episode, Mathew Embry speaks with Louise about searching for answers beyond her initial prognosis. She shares how she pursued treatments overseas.

programme.title: Along the Rio Grande
programme.desc: Cowboys (Tim Holt, Ray Whitley) catch a rustler when he crosses the Mexican border.

programme.title: Destry Rides Again
programme.desc: The small town of Bottleneck is under the control of Kent (Brian Donlevy), a power-hungry boss who gets control over the local cattle ranchers by winning a rigged game of cards. When the local sheriff questions the legitimacy of the game, Kent has him killed and names the town drunk, Dimsdale (Charles Winninger), as sheriff. What Kent doesn't know is that Dimsdale knows legendary lawman Tom Destry, who in turn sends his daring son Tom Destry Jr. (James Stewart) to Bottleneck to save the day.

programme.title: The Battle at Apache Pass
programme.desc: Indian leader Cochise (Jeff Chandler), whose wife (Susan Cabot) is pregnant, sides with a cavalry major (John Lund) against warring Geronimo.

programme.title: The Treasure of Pancho Villa
programme.desc: An American adventurer joins the supporters of Mexican revolutionary Pancho Villa in 1915, seeking excitement and purpose.

programme.title: The Dirty Dozen
programme.desc: As D-Day approaches, Colonel Breed hands the roguish Major Reisman an important assignment: He must train a team of soldiers to parachute across enemy lines and assassinate German personnel at a French château. The soldiers, recruited from murderers, rapists and criminals on death row, are promised commuted sentences. In spite of their history, the 12 men prove a spirited and courageous unit. Led by Major Reisman, they will exact revenge.

programme.title: Attack!
programme.desc: Lt. Joe Costa (Jack Palance) is a part of Fragile Fox Company, a National Guard unit sent to a Belgian town in the final stages of World War II. Costa harbors some serious doubts about the leadership abilities of Captain Cooney (Eddie Albert), a coward who only secured his position because of his ties to Lt. Col. Bartlett (Lee Marvin). Cooney's inexperience and his open contempt for Costa threaten the lives of his men as they draw closer to Battle of the Bulge.

programme.title: Riding Shotgun
programme.desc: A cowboy is hired to ride shotgun on a stagecoach, but his personal mission is to find a brutal killer and deliver justice. After the cowboy is lured from his post by a clue that might lead to the killer, he leaves the stagecoach unprotected.

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: More repulsive and venomous than any snake is the king of blackmailers.

programme.title: Paid Programming
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Springtime in the Sierras
programme.desc: A gang of poachers must be stopped as they kill and sell game out of season. Roy figures out who the culprits are and goes undercover to investigate further.

programme.title: Through the Bible With Les Feldick
programme.desc: Les Feldick, an Oklahoma farmer and rancher with a special gift for teaching the Bible; his simple and nondenominational approach on the program "Through the Bible with Les Feldick" has reached millions around the world with the Gospel of Grace.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Earp must keep a tight lid on problems in Dodge City while a journalist from New York pays a visit, but the job proves especially difficult on a chaotic day filled with dog fights, gunfire and rowdy cowboys.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: When a disgruntled cowhand holes up in a warehouse, Earp investigates the man's experiences in Dodge City to understand what drove him to violence and uncovers a chain of abuse perpetrated by underhanded citizens.

programme.title: Attack!
programme.desc: Lt. Joe Costa (Jack Palance) is a part of Fragile Fox Company, a National Guard unit sent to a Belgian town in the final stages of World War II. Costa harbors some serious doubts about the leadership abilities of Captain Cooney (Eddie Albert), a coward who only secured his position because of his ties to Lt. Col. Bartlett (Lee Marvin). Cooney's inexperience and his open contempt for Costa threaten the lives of his men as they draw closer to Battle of the Bulge.

programme.title: Stalag 17
programme.desc: One night in 1944 in a German POW camp housing American airmen, two prisoners try to escape the compound and are quickly discovered and shot dead. Among the remaining men, suspicion grows that one of their own is a spy for the Germans. All eyes fall on Sgt. Sefton who everybody knows frequently makes exchanges with German guards for small luxuries. To protect himself from a mob of his enraged fellow inmates, Sgt. Sefton resolves to find the true traitor within their midst.

programme.title: The Dirty Dozen
programme.desc: As D-Day approaches, Colonel Breed hands the roguish Major Reisman an important assignment: He must train a team of soldiers to parachute across enemy lines and assassinate German personnel at a French château. The soldiers, recruited from murderers, rapists and criminals on death row, are promised commuted sentences. In spite of their history, the 12 men prove a spirited and courageous unit. Led by Major Reisman, they will exact revenge.

programme.title: The Caine Mutiny
programme.desc: During World War II, a dilapidated vessel, the Caine, gets a new ensign, Willis Keith (Robert Francis), and a new captain, Commander Queeg (Humphrey Bogart). The crew sees Queeg's unconventional behavior as irrational, and communications officer Thomas Keefer (Fred MacMurray) spreads suspicion about his suitability as captain. When a dire situation during a storm forces the executive officer (Van Johnson) to relieve Queeg of his duties, he and Ensign Keith are tried for mutiny.

programme.title: The Best Years of Our Lives
programme.desc: Fred, Al and Homer are three World War II veterans facing difficulties as they re-enter civilian life. Fred (Dana Andrews) is a war hero who, unable to compete with more highly skilled workers, has to return to his low-wage soda jerk job. Bank executive Al (Fredric March) gets into trouble for offering favorable loans to veterans. After losing both hands in the war, Homer (Harold Russell) returns to his loving fiancée, but must struggle to adjust.

programme.title: Stalag 17
programme.desc: One night in 1944 in a German POW camp housing American airmen, two prisoners try to escape the compound and are quickly discovered and shot dead. Among the remaining men, suspicion grows that one of their own is a spy for the Germans. All eyes fall on Sgt. Sefton who everybody knows frequently makes exchanges with German guards for small luxuries. To protect himself from a mob of his enraged fellow inmates, Sgt. Sefton resolves to find the true traitor within their midst.

programme.title: The Fatal Mallet
programme.desc: Three men play dirty tricks to charm a beautiful young woman.

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The vicar of Amberley village asks Holmes to look into several deaths that have taken place since the arrival of a man said to be descended from vampires; though the village is gripped by fear, Holmes is convinced there is a rational explanation.

programme.title: Paid Programming
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Kansas Pacific
programme.desc: With the American Civil War looming, construction of the Pacific railroad is being held up in Kansas, sabotaged by Confederate sympathizers. The government dispatches an Army engineer, John Nelson (Sterling Hayden), to oversee construction, and he initially rubs the wrong way both his proud boss (Barton MacLane) and his boss's feisty daughter (Eve Miller). Eventually, though, both fall for Nelson's charms, and the hero seeks to uncover the Southern rat so he can lead the Union men due west.

programme.title: Through the Bible With Les Feldick
programme.desc: Les Feldick, an Oklahoma farmer and rancher with a special gift for teaching the Bible; his simple and nondenominational approach on the program "Through the Bible with Les Feldick" has reached millions around the world with the Gospel of Grace.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: When Dora Hand convinces Jim Kelley to sell his saloon in advance of their marriage, McGuire and Albright wrangle a deal to make Earp part-owner of the saloon without his knowledge and frame him for illegal business practices.

programme.title: Paid Programming: Carshield -- Cars Have Changed
The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Aggressive suitor Bob Rellance pesters Dora on the day before her marriage to Kelley, asking her to call off the wedding in favor of himself, but when tensions escalate into violence, Earp steps in to force Rellance out of town.

programme.title: The Caine Mutiny
programme.desc: During World War II, a dilapidated vessel, the Caine, gets a new ensign, Willis Keith (Robert Francis), and a new captain, Commander Queeg (Humphrey Bogart). The crew sees Queeg's unconventional behavior as irrational, and communications officer Thomas Keefer (Fred MacMurray) spreads suspicion about his suitability as captain. When a dire situation during a storm forces the executive officer (Van Johnson) to relieve Queeg of his duties, he and Ensign Keith are tried for mutiny.

programme.title: The Best Years of Our Lives
programme.desc: Fred, Al and Homer are three World War II veterans facing difficulties as they re-enter civilian life. Fred (Dana Andrews) is a war hero who, unable to compete with more highly skilled workers, has to return to his low-wage soda jerk job. Bank executive Al (Fredric March) gets into trouble for offering favorable loans to veterans. After losing both hands in the war, Homer (Harold Russell) returns to his loving fiancée, but must struggle to adjust.

programme.title: Little Women
programme.desc: The endearing saga of the March sisters -- Meg (Frances Dee), Jo (Katharine Hepburn), Amy and Beth -- who come of age in New England during the Civil War, is based on the classic Louisa May Alcott novel. With Mr. March away fighting for the Union cause, the girls and their beloved mother, Marmee (Spring Byington), manage to keep their spirits up through dire economic and emotional crises, until Jo's literary aspirations and Meg's romance with a teacher threaten to pull the sisters apart.

programme.title: The Nevadan
programme.desc: On his way to jail, bandit Tom Tanner (Forrest Tucker) escapes the law and is pursued by Andy Barclay (Randolph Scott), a federal marshal who believes that Tanner will lead him to a stash of stolen gold. Barclay changes horses at the ranch of Karen Galt (Dorothy Malone), planting the seeds of romance before following Tanner to a mine shaft where the stashed loot is hidden. But a gang of outlaws who also want the gold then arrives, forcing Tanner and Barclay to team up and fend them off together.

programme.title: The Longest Day
programme.desc: In 1944, the U.S. Army and Allied forces plan a huge invasion landing in Normandy, France. Despite bad weather, General Eisenhower gives the okay and the Allies land at Normandy. General Norman Cota travels with his men onto Omaha Beach. With much effort, and lost life, they get off the beach, traveling deep into French territory. The German military, due to arrogance, ignorance and a sleeping Adolf Hitler, delay their response to the Allied landing, with crippling results.

programme.title: The Bridges at Toko-Ri
programme.desc: Naval Reservist pilot Lt. Harry Brubaker (William Holden) is stationed on an aircraft carrier in the Sea of Japan during the Korean War. Although suffering from battle fatigue and longing to return to civilian life with his wife, Nancy (Grace Kelly), he agrees to fly a dangerous bombing mission over North Korea, with the support of his faithful rescue crew, Forney (Mickey Rooney) and Gamidge (Earl Holliman), and his sympathetic commanding officer, Commander Lee (Charles McGraw).

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Bat Masterson travels to Dodge City and gets involved in a gunfight that causes him to lose Earp's approval; after befriending gang member Nellie Wright, Bat tries to get word to Earp about an upcoming robbery but ends up at the gang's mercy.

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Henrietta Doran is a rich American who's agreed to wed Lord Robert St. Simon, widely regarded as the most eligible bachelor in England.

programme.title: Paid Programming
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Bandit Queen
programme.desc: A young woman (Barbara Britton) becomes a masked avenger after gold-grabbers kill her Spanish parents in old California.

programme.title: Through the Bible With Les Feldick
programme.desc: Les Feldick, an Oklahoma farmer and rancher with a special gift for teaching the Bible; his simple and nondenominational approach on the program "Through the Bible with Les Feldick" has reached millions around the world with the Gospel of Grace.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: A newly deputized Bat complicates an arrest by shooting perpetrator Billy Jordan after Billy ran out of bullets; while Earp and the deputies race to find a surgeon, Billy's family holds Bat hostage and threatens to take revenge if Billy dies.

programme.desc: A young man's devotion to a wild stallion pays off when they uncover a ring of horse thieves.

programme.title: The Nevadan
programme.desc: On his way to jail, bandit Tom Tanner (Forrest Tucker) escapes the law and is pursued by Andy Barclay (Randolph Scott), a federal marshal who believes that Tanner will lead him to a stash of stolen gold. Barclay changes horses at the ranch of Karen Galt (Dorothy Malone), planting the seeds of romance before following Tanner to a mine shaft where the stashed loot is hidden. But a gang of outlaws who also want the gold then arrives, forcing Tanner and Barclay to team up and fend them off together.

programme.title: Riding Shotgun
programme.desc: A cowboy is hired to ride shotgun on a stagecoach, but his personal mission is to find a brutal killer and deliver justice. After the cowboy is lured from his post by a clue that might lead to the killer, he leaves the stagecoach unprotected.

programme.title: Twelve O'Clock High
programme.desc: In 1942, an American Air Force unit stationed in England is plagued with morale problems until no-nonsense Brigadier General Frank Savage (Gregory Peck) assumes command. His tough leadership is initially resented by not only his pilots but his second-in-command (Hugh Marlowe), a West Point graduate and son of a general. But, with the help of a hotshot flying ace (Robert Patten) and a sympathetic administrator (Dean Jagger), the unit pulls together into a gung-ho fighting crew.

programme.title: Kelly's Heroes
programme.desc: In the midst of World War II, an array of colorful American soldiers gets inside information from a drunk German officer about millions of dollars worth of gold hidden on enemy soil. Kelly (Clint Eastwood), a private with the platoon, devises a plan to sneak past the German officers to steal the loot for his crew. They recruit more men and set their plan into action. Despite several casualties, the men are determined to press forward, even if it means striking a deal with the opposing army.

programme.title: Hellcats of the Navy
programme.desc: Captain Casey Abbott (Ronald Reagan) commands an American submarine in the Pacific during World War II. When a perilous mission to retrieve a new Japanese mine for study goes awry, he's forced to abandon a likable combat diver, Wes Barton (Harry Lauter). Abbott's orders to leave a man behind appear to be justified, but his second in command, Don Landon (Arthur Franz), believes he had ulterior motives: Barton had been competing for the attention of his love interest, Helen (Nancy Davis).

programme.title: The Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A dead young man's affair with a beautiful widow brings odd, dangerous events to his grandmother's house.

programme.title: 1776 - 2026 250th Anniversary Quarter Celebration Program
programme.desc: 2026 will be one of the most historic years ever in U.S. coinage; join your host Mike Mezack to learn about the incredible U.S. Quarter program and get a chance at an exclusive P&D Roll!

programme.title: The Cisco Kid in Old New Mexico
programme.desc: The Cisco Kid (Duncan Renaldo) and Pancho (Martin Garralaga) free a girl (Gwen Kenyon) accused of murder, then find the killer.

programme.title: Through the Bible With Les Feldick
programme.desc: Les Feldick, an Oklahoma farmer and rancher with a special gift for teaching the Bible; his simple and nondenominational approach on the program "Through the Bible with Les Feldick" has reached millions around the world with the Gospel of Grace.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: With Earp and Bassett out of town, George Morris' gang sacks Dodge City and rounds up all the remaining deputies except for Bat, who finds Earp and sneaks back into town to fight the gang with the help of local residents.

programme.title: Paid Programming
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Hellcats of the Navy
programme.desc: Captain Casey Abbott (Ronald Reagan) commands an American submarine in the Pacific during World War II. When a perilous mission to retrieve a new Japanese mine for study goes awry, he's forced to abandon a likable combat diver, Wes Barton (Harry Lauter). Abbott's orders to leave a man behind appear to be justified, but his second in command, Don Landon (Arthur Franz), believes he had ulterior motives: Barton had been competing for the attention of his love interest, Helen (Nancy Davis).

programme.title: Twelve O'Clock High
programme.desc: In 1942, an American Air Force unit stationed in England is plagued with morale problems until no-nonsense Brigadier General Frank Savage (Gregory Peck) assumes command. His tough leadership is initially resented by not only his pilots but his second-in-command (Hugh Marlowe), a West Point graduate and son of a general. But, with the help of a hotshot flying ace (Robert Patten) and a sympathetic administrator (Dean Jagger), the unit pulls together into a gung-ho fighting crew.

programme.title: Kelly's Heroes
programme.desc: In the midst of World War II, an array of colorful American soldiers gets inside information from a drunk German officer about millions of dollars worth of gold hidden on enemy soil. Kelly (Clint Eastwood), a private with the platoon, devises a plan to sneak past the German officers to steal the loot for his crew. They recruit more men and set their plan into action. Despite several casualties, the men are determined to press forward, even if it means striking a deal with the opposing army.

programme.title: Riding Shotgun
programme.desc: A cowboy is hired to ride shotgun on a stagecoach, but his personal mission is to find a brutal killer and deliver justice. After the cowboy is lured from his post by a clue that might lead to the killer, he leaves the stagecoach unprotected.

programme.title: The Hanging Tree
programme.desc: Joseph "Doc" Frail (Gary Cooper) is a doctor with a past he's trying to outrun. While in Montana, he comes across a mining camp with a hanging tree and rescues a man named Rune (Ben Piazza) from the noose. With Rune as his servant, Frail decides to settle down, and he takes over as town doctor. He meets Elizabeth (Maria Schell), who is suffering from shock, and the two soon fall in love. But when Elizabeth is attacked, Frail's attempt to help her land them both in trouble.

programme.title: Operation Pacific
programme.desc: In the South Pacific, submarine officer Duke Gifford (John Wayne) and the crew of the "Thunderfish" are attacked by the Japanese during a rescue mission, and their torpedoes malfunction. Returning safely to Pearl Harbor, Duke meets his ex-wife, Mary (Patricia Neal), who still feels strongly for him but is engaged to a pilot, the brother of Duke's commander, Pop Perry (Ward Bond). Determined to win Mary back, Duke is sidetracked when he and the crew are ordered on a mission with new torpedoes.

programme.title: Premier League Soccer
programme.desc: Crystal Palace hosts in a regular-season English Premier League matchup. Forward Jean-Philippe Mateta leads Crystal Palace against forward Viktor Gyokeres and the first-place Arsenal squad as the clubs battle today at Selhurst Park.

programme.title: 1914 Gold Rare Coin
programme.desc: Add the stunning Set of 2 1914 & 1915 San Francisco Saint Gaudens Gold Double Eagle in Mint State 64 condition to your numismatic collection. Of all 1914 and 1915-S Gaudens Struck by the US Mint, less than 1% have been graded MS64.

programme.title: Bathroom Makeover
programme.desc: Transform your bath affordably in just one day!

programme.title: Salute to Veterans
programme.desc: The Salute to Veterans program is in tribute to our military service men and women on Independence Day. Thanks for supporting our 22 million veterans and their families. We greatly appreciate your support.

programme.title: Rare 1920's Silver
programme.desc: Add a 10-Coin or 20-Coin Roll of the iconic Peace Silver Dollars from 1922-1925 in Brilliant Uncirculated condition to your collection. Honoring the peace achieved after WWI, these Classic American Silver coins boast historical significance.

programme.title: Shark Tank
programme.desc: NBA champion Bill Walton helps a triathlete pitch his idea for a unique water bottle; a ghostwriter from California seeks a business investment; two women from Minnesota present their online business that helps people plan their own funerals.

programme.title: Shark Tank
programme.desc: Singer Brian McKnight and a DJ pitch a syndicated radio show featuring love song dedications; skateboards that glide like surfboards; patching holes in window and door screens; an update on the creator of a unique belt.

programme.title: Shark Tank
programme.desc: A device to eliminate clogged sinks; a real grass delivery service for pets without a yard; a way to share lip balm, but not germs; update on artisan cheese and glazed donut sandwich business, Tom & Chee.

programme.title: Shark Tank
programme.desc: Former Google executive Chris Sacca is a guest shark; two sisters pitch a tech education business; a specialized version of a popular home-rental website; a unique twist on a popular hot drink; a device that can inflate objects in seconds.

programme.title: Shark Tank
programme.desc: High heels that are sexy and comfortable; a solution for a common food storage issue; a stylish solution to a common frustration with women's bathing suits; a successful sauce company.

programme.title: Shark Tank
programme.desc: Emma Grede, the CEO of Good American, guest sharks; a napping product designed to reduce stress; a product which allows kids to unleash their creativity without the mess; a line of cozy accessories for the great outdoors.

programme.title: Shark Tank
programme.desc: An AI tool that offers cost-friendly college admissions support; a Japanese persimmon-based soap; edible candy crafts for kids; sleeping bags made exclusively for children.

programme.title: Shark Tank
programme.desc: A handheld personal groomer; a picturesque alternative to a cat perch; scented nasal diffusers; gourmet mozzarella sticks one can make at home.

programme.title: Shark Tank
programme.desc: Former Google executive Chris Sacca is a guest shark; two sisters pitch a tech education business; a specialized version of a popular home-rental website; a unique twist on a popular hot drink; a device that can inflate objects in seconds.

programme.title: Shark Tank
programme.desc: A device to eliminate clogged sinks; a real grass delivery service for pets without a yard; a way to share lip balm, but not germs; update on artisan cheese and glazed donut sandwich business, Tom & Chee.

programme.title: Shark Tank
programme.desc: Emma Grede, the CEO of Good American, guest sharks; a napping product designed to reduce stress; a product which allows kids to unleash their creativity without the mess; a line of cozy accessories for the great outdoors.

programme.title: America's Next Investment
programme.desc: America's Next Investment: Exploring Opportunities in AI,Tech, Real Estate, Alcohol, Cannabis, and More. Tune in to discover how to invest!

programme.title: America's Next Investment
programme.desc: America's Next Investment: Exploring Opportunities in AI,Tech, Real Estate, Alcohol, Cannabis, and More. Tune in to discover how to invest!

programme.title: CNBC Sport: On the Record
programme.desc: Conversations with ESPN Host Stephen A. Smith, New York Mets President of Baseball Operations David Stearns, TMRW Sports co-founder and CEO Mike McCarley.

programme.title: CNBC Sport: On the Record
programme.desc: Conversations with Chicago Cubs Owner Tom Ricketts, Kansas City Chiefs Chairman and CEO Clark Hunt and horse trainer Bob Baffert.

programme.title: Squawk Box Europe
programme.desc: "Squawk Box Europe" provides three hours of analysis and debate on trading days to lead up to the open of the European markets.

programme.title: CNBC Cures: Defying Rare Disease
programme.desc: CNBC's Becky Quick steps out from behind the anchor desk to share her family's private battle with her daughter's rare disease; through her story and others like it, Quick reveals the challenges facing families trapped between breakthrough science and funding gaps.

programme.title: Leader's Playbook
programme.desc: This episode of Leader's Playbook takes you inside Netflix, the number one streaming platform in the world. In this candid interview with CEO Ted Sarandos, CNBC's Julia Boorstin explores the CEO's leadership playbook to understand the strategies that changed what we watch and how we watch it. This insightful conversation covers it all from Sarandos' journey from video store manager to global hit maker and how the leader empowers his teams to succeed.

programme.title: Leader's Playbook
programme.desc: This episode of Leader's Playbook takes you inside Shake Shack, one of the fastest growing restaurant chains in America; in this candid interview with founder Danny Meyer, CNBC's Julia Boorstin explores Meyer's leadership playbook to reveal the strategies that helped the serial entrepreneur turn a hot dog cart in NYC into an international burger empire; the insightful conversation covers it all from his leadership secret sauce to what he looks for in every new hire.

programme.title: Leader's Playbook
programme.desc: This episode of Leader's Playbook takes you inside Uber the #1 ride-sharing platform in the world. In this candid interview with CEO Dara Khosrowshahi, CNBC's Julia Boorstin explores the CEO's leadership playbook to reveal the strategies that helped him overcome challenges, drive change and future-proof the business. The insightful conversation covers it all from turning around a toxic culture to the future of self-driving cars. Find out the valuable lesson Khosrowshahi learned.

programme.title: Leader's Playbook
programme.desc: This episode of Leader's Playbook takes you inside the billionaire mindset of Barry Diller, the media legend who launched FOX and built internet brands used by millions. In this candid interview with Diller, CNBC's Julia Boorstin explores the media mogul's leadership playbook to reveal the strategies that powered six decades of success across television, film, travel websites and even dating apps.

programme.desc: Fortified, sugar-free nut butters; an acupressure wrist band; an at-home tattoo removal device; an update on inventors who created surf-inspired fitness equipment.

programme.title: Shark Tank

programme.title: Shark Tank
programme.desc: Guest shark Ashton Kutcher; a device to make baby's feeding time easier; wearable transportation; drinking vinegars; protecting consumers from hacking; an update on Pipcorn.

programme.title: Shark Tank

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp

programme.desc: Earp rides to the scene of a stagecoach robbery and follows tracks to find a woman, the sole survivor of the attack, in shock; Earp must shoot his way out of a cave when the bandits trap him inside with the woman.

programme.desc: Earp rides into Santa Fe, N.M., to stop a war between two families vying for control of the land, the Finneys and the Grangers; the local sheriff backs the duplicitous Finneys, who created fake land deeds, but Earp finds the real deeds.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: When a mysterious man helps Earp with injured stagecoach passengers, Earp takes him to Dodge City, introduces him to Dr. McCarty and talks him into opening a medical practice; a smallpox outbreak leads to a disturbing suspicion about the new doctor.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: A newspaper editor facing backlash for baseless accusations toward dance hall owner Sally Bascom kills her and frames Earp for the murder; Earp plays along to lure the editor into a trap while Doc Holliday and Gibbs take matters into their own hands.

programme.title: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
programme.desc: Doc Holliday's friend holds up a stage carrying Earp's younger brother, Morgan, who finds Doc and tells him what happened; suspecting trouble, Doc and Morgan team up to find his friend and rescue him from a gang without notifying Earp.

programme.title: Weekend Recharge
programme.desc: Though it has "weekend" in the title, this show discusses the week ahead in addition to offering suggestions for what to do on Saturday and Sunday. Weather Channel meteorologists go beyond the elements outside and into the realm of lifestyle tips. Each host has a recurring segment on "Weekend Recharge," which includes helping parents get a fresh start to each day and explaining how the weather is expected to impact the weekend's scheduled sports action.

programme.title: Pattrn
programme.desc: Elevating the conversation about climate, extreme weather, sustainability and green living.

programme.title: Joplin: 15 Years Later
programme.desc: Meteorologist Mike Bettes returns to the site of the deadly EF5 tornado in Missouri, one of the most destructive in American history.

programme.title: Storm Stories: The Next Chapter
programme.desc: A Dallas man who heads outside to get a pizza during halftime of a Cowboys-Eagles football game spots an EF-3 tornado barreling through the city; a trauma nurse jumps into action as a rare Christmastime twister devastates a small Mississippi town.

programme.title: Storm Stories: The Next Chapter
programme.desc: A couple and their four children are forced to abandon their pickup in the midst of ferocious floods outside Baton Rouge, La., while the parents of a 22-year-old daughter search for her in the rubble of a deadly tornado outbreak in southern Illinois.

programme.title: Uncharted Adventure
programme.desc: Mike begins his uncharted exploration of Latin America in Mexico; he focuses on the Yucatán region known for its stunning beaches, Mayan ruins and captivating indigenous heritage.

programme.title: Uncharted Adventure
programme.desc: The Latin American journey continues with Costa Rica, a nation with many awe-inspiring national parks and full of thriving wildlife; Mike discovers why this welcoming nation has been ranked as one of the happiest places in the world.

programme.title: Uncharted Adventure
programme.desc: Over his five days in Peru, Mike witnesses the environmental diversity of the country; surrounded by nature and humbled by the Peruvian hospitality, he gets to experience all the adventures the lush green land has to offer.

programme.title: Joplin: 15 Years Later
programme.desc: Meteorologist Mike Bettes returns to the site of the deadly EF5 tornado in Missouri, one of the most destructive in American history.

programme.title: Claw Hunters
programme.desc: Mack brings his old man on a trip; Greg hauls all of his traps to prep for spring; Gail makes her biggest sale of the season.

programme.title: Heroes & Survivors
programme.desc: Winds whip up a deadly wildfire that torches a town; ferocious ocean waves leave storm watchers stranded; white water rafters are pummeled by rain and rocks during a trip down the Grand Canyon.

programme.title: Heroes & Survivors
programme.desc: A microburst in Mexico brings down a plane; a rare tornado outbreak catches an entire town off guard; alone in the Atlantic, a 70-year-old solo rower battles a fierce ocean storm; wild white waters sweep away a hiker.

programme.title: Heroes & Survivors
programme.desc: A winter pile-up explodes into chaos; ferocious floodwaters take a driver on a terrifying ride of a lifetime; airboats come to the rescue during Hurricane Florence; friends must save a skier buried alive.

programme.title: Hurricane 360
programme.desc: Hurricane Katrina turned 49 Mississippi counties into disaster zones.

programme.title: Hurricane 360
programme.desc: Rising seawater comes in contact with electrical wires at a residence, setting off a six-alarm fire that engulfs nearly 130 houses; the second-biggest fire in New York City history.

programme.title: Weird Earth
programme.desc: Flammable snow could be an international danger; black smoke with a mind of its own; a giant red dome that appears to defy the laws of physics.

programme.title: Weird Earth
programme.desc: A giant ice stack on a remote lake spouts a strange vapor, dazzling jeweled objects litter a dank cave floor and a man's shout causes a lake to bizarrely react with thousands of ripples.

programme.title: Weird Earth
programme.desc: Threatening blue lights bombard a famous resort town, evoking the apocalypse, a nuclear meltdown could be behind an ominous blazing beam of light and supersized, underground crystals appear out of this world.

programme.desc: Starting the day by opening a window into weather, looking out at the stories and science behind the forecast.

programme.title: Jerry & Marge Go Large
programme.desc: (2022) Fact-based comedy starring Bryan Cranston and Annette Bening as a retired couple who find a way to exploit a lottery loophole. But then some rich kids try to muscle in...

programme.title: Entrapment
programme.desc: (1999) Action thriller starring Sean Connery as Robert 'Mac' MacDougal, one of the world's finest art thieves, and Catherine Zeta-Jones as the insurance company agent out to trap him.

programme.title: Angel Has Fallen
programme.desc: (2019) Action-thriller sequel. Secret agent Mike Banning (Gerard Butler) goes on the run when he's framed for an attack on the President. Can he clear his name?

programme.title: 3:10 to Yuma
programme.desc: (2007) Western starring Christian Bale and Russell Crowe. An impoverished rancher tries to earn some money and his family's respect by escorting a dangerous outlaw on his way to jail.

programme.title: Corpus Christi
programme.desc: (2019) Crime drama. A young ex-con wants to be a priest so much that he pretends to be one. But trouble soon finds him. In Polish/subs. Strong language/violence/sex/drugs.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: LumoTV: DeSign
programme.desc: Shiv's outgrown his bedroom - can the team create an independent space fit for a growing Deaf boy?

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Guadalcanal Diary
programme.desc: (1943) Second World War action drama following two US marines as they make advances into the Solomon Islands to drive back the Japanese forces. Starring Preston Foster and Lloyd Nolan.

programme.title: Man in the Saddle
programme.desc: (1951) Western. Randolph Scott stars as a rancher who's the target of unwelcome harassment by his richer and more powerful neighbour. With Alexander Knox, Joan Leslie and Ellen Drew.

programme.title: Turner & Hooch
programme.desc: (1989) Comedy-thriller starring Tom Hanks. A cop investigates a murder to which there's just one witness: Hooch, a slobbering, beer-drinking French mastiff, who now needs 24/7 protection.

programme.title: Robot Dreams
programme.desc: (2023) Pablo Berger's debut animation is the endearing tale of a lonely dog who builds himself a robot friend and a relationship that helps him unlock the joys of 1980s New York City.

programme.title: Notting Hill
programme.desc: (1999) Hugh Grant and Julia Roberts star in the BAFTA-winning comedy romance about a bookshop owner who manages to get a glamorous movie star to go out with him. With Rhys Ifans.

programme.title: Groundhog Day
programme.desc: (1993) Harold Ramis's classic déjà-vu comedy starring Bill Murray. A TV weatherman must relive the same day reporting on a clairvoyant groundhog until he learns to behave himself.

programme.title: The Pope's Exorcist
programme.desc: (2023) Supernatural horror starring Russell Crowe, loosely based on the memoirs of a 'real' exorcist. A case of demonic possession in Spain connects to dark secrets from the Inquisition.

programme.title: Stage Mother
programme.desc: (2020) Feel-good comedy-drama with Jacki Weaver. Two worlds collide when a small-town, conservative choir director inherits a drag club in San Francisco from her estranged son.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Lifeboat
programme.desc: (1944) Alfred Hitchcock's Oscar-nominated Second World War drama starring Tallulah Bankhead, William Bendix and Walter Slezak as three of the survivors from a liner torpedoed by the Nazis.

programme.title: This Gun for Hire
programme.desc: (1942) Classic noir starring Alan Ladd and Veronica Lake. As a cold-blooded assassin seek vengeance for a double cross, he unwisely falls for the girlfriend of the cop who's pursuing him.

programme.title: Yellow Sky
programme.desc: (1948) Western. Gregory Peck plays the leader of an outlaw gang whose plans to rob an old man and his granddaughter of their gold go seriously awry. With Anne Baxter and Richard Widmark.

programme.title: The Rare Breed
programme.desc: (1966) Western with Maureen O'Hara and James Stewart. With the help of a drifter, a widow strives to fulfil her husband's last wish to introduce rare cattle to the perilous American west.

programme.title: Charlotte Gray
programme.desc: (2001) World War II thriller. Cate Blanchett stars as a secret agent sent to occupied France to aid the Resistance against the Nazis. But she has her own agenda. With Michael Gambon.

programme.title: Prey
programme.desc: (2022) It's Predator, 1719 style. Amber Midthunder stars as a Comanche hunter who must save her tribe from an extraterrestrial threat in this action prequel. If it bleeds, you can kill it.

programme.title: Renfield
programme.desc: (2023) Comedy horror vampire shenanigans with Nicholas Hoult, Awkwafina and a full-blooded Nicolas Cage. Dracula's familiar gets distracted from procuring food for his master.

programme.title: Donkey Punch
programme.desc: (2008) Thriller. A group of British teenagers are partying aboard a luxury yacht in the Med when a sexual encounter ends in manslaughter... and provokes a life-or-death fight for survival.

programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Shadow of a Doubt
programme.desc: (1943) Hitchcock thriller starring Joseph Cotten as a serial killer fleeing the law. Teresa Wright is his adoring niece who, initially, refuses to believe her uncle is a murderer.

programme.title: The Spoilers
programme.desc: (1955) Western. Anne Baxter, Jeff Chandler and Rory Calhoun star in a tale of fortune-seeking and treachery on the untamed Alaskan frontier.

programme.title: The Yellow Mountain
programme.desc: (1954) Western. Lex Barker and Howard Duff star as prospectors whose friendship is tested when a promising seam of the gold is discovered in a Nevada mountain. With Mala Powers.

programme.title: Shenandoah
programme.desc: (1965) Western. James Stewart stars as a wealthy Virginia farming patriarch trying to keep his family together and safe in the midst of the encroaching American Civil War.

programme.title: Our Friend
programme.desc: (2019) Dakota Johnson, Jason Segel and Casey Affleck in Gabriela Cowperthwaite's poignant drama about a family coping with a terminal cancer diagnosis, and the friend who moves in to help.

programme.title: East Is East
programme.desc: (1999) Comedy with Jimi Mistry and Om Puri. A Pakistani chippy owner tries to raise his kids amid cultural tensions in 1970s Salford.

programme.title: Fury
programme.desc: (2014) World War II action with Brad Pitt. A tank commander leads his crew into the dark, bloody heart of Hitler's last stand. But there's a rookie on board the Fury. With Michael Peña.

programme.title: Dreamland
programme.desc: (2019) Depression-era thriller with Margot Robbie. A young boy agrees to help an injured fugitive who's hiding out in an abandoned barn where he goes to read.

programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Arizona Raiders
programme.desc: (1965) William Whitney's brutal western. Two former Confederate rebels are offered their freedom if they can track, catch or even kill their former gang mates. Starring Audie Murphy.

programme.title: Hail the Conquering Hero
programme.desc: (1944) Satirical wartime comedy starring Eddie Bracken as a man who becomes a celebrated war hero despite having been medically discharged from the marines after only a few weeks.

programme.title: The Nevadan
programme.desc: (1950) Western with Randolph Scott and Dorothy Malone. A cunning US Marshal pretends to be a doofus to get a bank robber to take him to the hidden loot.

programme.title: Local Hero
programme.desc: (1983) Comedy starring Peter Riegert, Burt Lancaster and Peter Capaldi. A US oil company executive tries to persuade a small Scottish seaside community to sell up their entire village.

programme.title: Entrapment
programme.desc: (1999) Action thriller starring Sean Connery as Robert 'Mac' MacDougal, one of the world's finest art thieves, and Catherine Zeta-Jones as the insurance company agent out to trap him.

programme.title: Sumotherhood
programme.desc: (2023) Adam Deacon's cameo-packed send-up of gritty noughties London crime dramas. Two wannabe gangsters try to make it big in the hood by robbing a bank. What could go wrong?

programme.title: Dazed and Confused
programme.desc: (1993) Richard Linklater's school's-out-forever comedy-drama with Ben Affleck and Milla Jovovich. Texan students let loose to celebrate their freedom in the summer of 1976.

programme.title: How to Blow Up a Pipeline
programme.desc: (2022) High-octane eco-thriller. A disparate group of young Americans unite to disrupt the oil industry in an act motivated by everything from the ideological to the downright personal.

programme.title: An Eye for an Eye
programme.desc: Ex-bounty hunter Talion (Robert Lansing) comes home one day to find his house ablaze and his entire family slaughtered. He soon learns that the culprits are a gang led by nasty Ike Slant (Slim Pickens). While tracking the outlaws, Talion meets fellow bounty hunter Benny (Pat Wayne), who's also after Ike. Initially mistrustful of each other, Benny and Talion are forced to work together when, during a shootout with the criminals, the latter is blinded and the former injures his shooting hand.

programme.title: Maverick
programme.desc: Bret loses several thousand dollars to a beautiful young girl who defies the law of averages.

programme.title: The Virginian
programme.desc: Outlaws hold stage passengers, including Garth and Betsy, and will kill them all if one doesn't become a sacrifice by sundown; guests Skip Homeier, Richard Anderson.

programme.title: F Troop
programme.desc: A Sicilian chef challenges Agarn when Agarn goes after his beloved daughter.

programme.title: Cheyenne
programme.desc: Cheyenne helps two prospectors find a gold mine.

programme.title: Maverick
programme.desc: Maverick's traveling companion disappears.

programme.title: Stagecoach
programme.desc: John Ford's landmark Western revolves around an assorted group of colorful passengers aboard the Overland stagecoach bound for Lordsburg, New Mexico, in the 1880s. An alcoholic philosophizer (Thomas Mitchell), a lady of ill repute (Claire Trevor) and a timid liquor salesman (Donald Meek) are among the motley crew of travelers who must contend with an escaped outlaw, the Ringo Kid (John Wayne), and the ever-present threat of an Apache attack as they make their way across the Wild West.

programme.title: Gunsmoke
programme.desc: Kitty endangers her life when a man seeks revenge on a rancher.

programme.title: Gunsmoke
programme.desc: Matt is pressured by a railroad representative to solve the death of a baggage clerk that occurred during the robbery of a train.

programme.title: Gunsmoke
programme.desc: Matt pursues an outlaw gang blowing up safes with nitroglycerin.

programme.title: Gunsmoke
programme.desc: A lovesick, penniless transient embarks on a lucrative but perilous career, boiling nitroglycerine off dynamite for a gang of robbers.

programme.title: Have Gun -- Will Travel
programme.desc: Paladin defends a man he finds chained like a dog to a stake.

programme.title: Have Gun -- Will Travel
programme.desc: Paladin (Richard Boone) and a remorseful robber break into a bank to return a gold bar. Also starring John Fiedler.

programme.title: The Virginian
programme.desc: A South American (Ricardo Montalban) uses armed men and barbed wire around his land, which is surrounded by Shiloh property.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Newspaperman Byron Claremont specializes in profiles of gunmen; one of his stories is responsible for an old-time marshal's death; and his next subject is Lucas.

programme.title: Cheyenne
programme.desc: Cheyenne is forced into a gunfight with the Laverson brothers, leaving one dead and one out for revenge.

programme.title: Daniel Boone
programme.desc: Boone employs a mastodon's skeleton to fight two slave traders seeking runaway slaves.

programme.desc: With Dr. Mike Murdock.

programme.desc: With Christian success coach Dr. Dave Martin.

programme.title: Gunsmoke
programme.desc: A man who saved Matt's life 10 years earlier arrives in Dodge City.

programme.title: The Big Valley
programme.desc: A Spanish nobleman threatens to foreclose on ranchers when Heath falls for his daughter (Katharine Ross).

programme.title: Laramie
programme.desc: While visiting Laramie, Daisy discovers a dead body on the floor down the hall from her hotel room, but she has trouble getting people to believe her when she identifies the killer.

programme.title: Cheyenne
programme.desc: Cheyenne uses an old theatrical trick to pin down a confession from a clever murderer.

programme.desc: An Italian count arrives in North Fork and challenges the town bully to a duel.

programme.desc: A new bank in North Fork is in danger of being robbed.

programme.title: Maverick

programme.desc: A woman finally learns the truth about her son that she hasn't seen in 10 years. Guest star: Agnes Moorehead. With Ward Bond and Robert Horton.

programme.title: Gunsmoke
programme.desc: Matt tries to capture bandits who rob a stagecoach on which Matt and Chester are riding.

programme.desc: Matt is reluctant to make an arrest when a friend is accused of murder.

programme.title: Cheyenne
programme.desc: Ben Creed (Robert Wilke) plots a staged robbery of Cheyenne so he can regain the foreman's loyalty.

programme.title: Have Gun -- Will Travel
programme.desc: One man's will causes three gunmen to murder each other instead of their intended victim, Paladin (Richard Boone). Also starring George Kennedy.

programme.desc: An Eastern photographer who will do anything for a picture enters sacred Indian land.

programme.desc: Ruthless gunman Mannon (Steve Forrest) shoots Festus, steals his mule and terrorizes the citizens of Dodge City.

programme.desc: Disguised as a layman, a troubled priest (Michael Parks) joins Chris Hale's wagon train in an attempt to forget his past. Guest star: Dick York.

programme.desc: A bounty hunter (Gregg Palmer) waylays Trampas then takes him to a town where he is mistakenly identified as the killer of a beloved local doctor; guests Dennis Cooney, Beverlee McKinsey.

programme.desc: A young man helps Jim investigate a robbery.

programme.desc: Trust God for your breakthrough. Enter into powerful worship! Join us for Campmeeting.

programme.desc: A man is stabbed after winning a poker game, and the wrong man may have been arrested.

programme.desc: Jess saves a crippled Blackfoot youth from a scalp hunter despite the Army edict that forbids settlers from helping a member of the Blackfoot tribe.

programme.desc: Cheyenne works undercover as a ranch hand to prevent the theft of a cattle empire.

programme.desc: Mark tries to play Cupid for his father and a pretty young stranger who arrives in town.

programme.desc: Major Adams is forced to come to the rescue of a fast-talking con artist who gets himself into a situation that he can't handle.

programme.desc: In order to save a priest from death, Paladin (Richard Boone) must shoot a man he just rescued. Also starring John Mauldin.

programme.desc: A trigger-happy homesteader claims the reward for shooting a wanted criminal but then learns that he shot the wrong man.

programme.desc: Two hillbillies attempt to swindle the townspeople of Dodge City by selling them worthless mining claims.

programme.desc: A respected rancher is found to have been a notoriously cruel Civil War commandant.

Ben has his three sons, each with a different (and deceased) mother. programme.title: Justice With Judge Mablean
programme.desc: Judge Mablean Ephriam, who presided over "Divorce Court" from 1999-2006 as the first star of the revived version of the show, returns to the courtroom genre with his half-hour series that deals with life and the law. programme.title: The Verdict With Judge Hatchett
programme.desc: Judge Glenda Hatchett presided over her self-titled courtroom show from 2000-2008. Now, she returns to the medium with "The Verdict." In the half-hour series, the Atlanta native and former juvenile-court judge rules over small-claims cases brought in front of her. Hatchett's courtroom isn't just about finding someone guilty, though. She also uses her experience dealing with young people and her tough-love approach to help send a message to out-of-control juvenile offenders to try to set them on the right track in their lives.

programme.title: Supreme Justice With Judge Karen
programme.desc: Karen Mills was a county court judge and criminal defense attorney in Florida before turning to TV to preside over her own court shows. In her third effort, "Supreme Justice," Mills tackles small-claims court arbitrations in her compassionate way, to which viewers of her previous shows have grown accustomed. As usual with court shows, cases involve friendships gone bad, money disputes and property damage.
